name: CROSSGATE MOOR
units: fathoms/feet/inches, transcribed verbatim — convert ONLY downstream

annotations: both feet cells carry a stray diagonal pen-stroke; value 3 confirmed by arithmetic (cum. 27 ft; reconciles with rows 3 and 5)
depth inches cell obscured by an ink blot; value 8 in. confirmed by arithmetic (cum. 49 ft 8 in)
'dant' = soft sooty inferior coal (Durham vernacular); word could also be read 'daunt/daut' but 'dant' is the standard term
5-in. carbonaceous layer sandwiched between boulder stones — detrital coaly debris in the buried-valley fill, not an in-situ seam; no seam name given
2-in. parting within the drift sequence — not an in-situ seam, no seam name

intervals.verbatim: Fine brown sand, with water at 121' 6".
annotations: with water at 121 ft 6 in (as written "121' 6\""); the water strike lies within this 57-ft interval (75 ft 6 in to 132 ft)

intervals.verbatim: Dark stony clay, with sand partings

annotations: with water; hole bottomed in this bed ('into' = boring stopped within it)
faint pencil mark after 'into' in the description column (looks like '7·3'), illegible — not a seam name

notes: Single-page log (p01) plus a blank pre-printed continuation sheet (p02 — Thickness / Depth-from-Surface column headers only, no strata; scanner noise and two ink blots). Header as written: 'SECTION OF Borehole near Crofton Holme (Fernhill), Crossgate Moor.' ('Crofton Holme' and '(Fernhill)' are probable readings of a difficult hand). NGR NZ 2616 4271; Surface Level c. 300' O.D.; Communicated by 'BGS 2651' (probable, likely a file/register number); Date of boring or sinking 1901; Borer 'Wm. Coulson' (William Coulson & Sons, the well-known Durham well-boring firm); One-inch Map 26; Six-inch Durham 27 NW; BGS Registration No. NZ 24 SE 27, Page No. 1. Entire hole is drift — buried-valley fill of clays, sands, gravel and boulder stones (Crossgate Moor sits over the buried channel), with two thin coaly 'dant' layers (rows 12 and 14) that carry no seam name and are detrital rather than in-situ; no solid Coal Measures rock reached, and no pencil seam-correlation marginalia (LM/HM/Md/HTN etc.) present. Clerk arithmetic cross-checks CLEAN on all 22 intervals (previous depth + thickness = written depth throughout, no slips). Final depth 33 fathoms 5 ft 0 in = 203 ft = 61.87 m, matching the BGS index depth. '52' and '27' at top right are registration/index numbering, not log content; the black shapes in the left margin and columns are ink blots (row 11 inches and row 4 feet recovered by arithmetic).
